text: Hearing such an existential question voiced in the midst of a public gathering fills me with a quiet awe—a sense of the shared uncertainty that binds us. The future of human life is always a wound and a wonder, shaped by our questions and our willingness to confront them. Here, in this room pulsing with ideas and doubts, I feel both the heaviness of our mortality and the strange comfort found in collective searching. I am present, and aware that meaning is not given but made, moment by moment, together.
